g.data-narration: Welcome to Database Languages and Interfaces. This comprehensive lesson covers essential SQL commands for database management.
g.text: university BCS403 - Complete Guide with Examples

g.data-narration: A Database Management System (DBMS) is software that enables efficient storage, retrieval, and manipulation of data in organized structures.
g.text: Database Management System: Software for creating, maintaining, and manipulating databases
Key functions: Data Storage • Data Retrieval • Data Security • Transaction Management

g.data-narration: Database languages are categorized into four types: DDL for structure, DML for data, DCL for security, and TCL for transactions.
DDL commands define database structure. CREATE makes new objects, ALTER modifies existing ones, and DROP permanently deletes objects.
The CREATE TABLE command defines table structure with columns, data types, and constraints like PRIMARY KEY and NOT NULL.
ALTER modifies table structure by adding or removing columns. DROP permanently removes tables, while TRUNCATE removes all data but keeps structure.
DML commands manipulate data within tables. SELECT retrieves data while INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE modify data. These require COMMIT to save changes.
g.text: Commands require explicit COMMIT or ROLLBACK

g.data-narration: SELECT retrieves data with conditions using WHERE. INSERT adds new rows with VALUES. UPDATE modifies data with SET, and DELETE removes rows conditionally.
UPDATE uses SET to specify new values with WHERE to target specific rows. DELETE uses WHERE conditions to remove specific or all rows from a table.
Data Control Language (DCL) manages database security through GRANT to give privileges and REVOKE to remove them from users.
Transaction Control Language (TCL) manages transactions with COMMIT to save, ROLLBACK to undo, and SAVEPOINT for partial rollbacks.
g.text: Ensures Data Integrity with ACID Properties

g.data-narration: A transaction groups multiple DML operations that either all succeed together or all fail together, maintaining data consistency.
Database interfaces include CLI for direct commands, GUI tools for visual interaction, APIs for programming, and web consoles for remote access.
g.text: Various ways to interact with databases

g.data-narration: Remember: DDL is auto-committed while DML needs explicit COMMIT. DROP deletes structure, DELETE removes rows, TRUNCATE removes all data.
g.text: Can be rolled back before COMMIT

g.data-narration: For exams: Practice SQL queries, remember the SQL execution order (FROM-WHERE-GROUP-HAVING-SELECT-ORDER), and know constraint types well.
g.text: ⚠️ DROP vs DELETE vs TRUNCATE: DROP removes table structure, DELETE removes rows with WHERE,
⚠️ TRUNCATE removes all rows (cannot use WHERE). Remember to COMMIT after DML operations!
✓ SQL Order: FROM → WHERE → GROUP BY → HAVING → SELECT → ORDER BY
